gene_region.label: SI transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical SI genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: SI encodes sucrase-isomaltase, an intestinal brush-border enzyme for sucrose and starch-derived disaccharide digestion. This topic bundle emphasizes digestion and gut barrier biology, with interpretation centered on intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local SI bundle is oriented toward digestion and gut barrier biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: SI variants can alter, tag, or contextualize intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Digestion, malabsorption, gut barrier, and microbiome interaction context
Inflammatory bowel disease and mucosal immune pathway biology
Nutrient transport, digestive enzyme, and epithelial repair review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: SI methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for SI.
Because SI biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use SI promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling.
Digestion, malabsorption, gut barrier, and microbiome interaction context
Inflammatory bowel disease and mucosal immune pathway biology

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000090402: SI coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: SI biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: SI: digestion and gut barrier biology via intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SI digestion and gut barrier biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: SI digestion and gut barrier biology pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: SI gut-digestion variation model is curated as a SI marker for Digestion, malabsorption, gut barrier, and microbiome interaction context, Inflammatory bowel disease and mucosal immune pathway biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag intestinal epithelial barrier function, digestion and nutrient transport, mucus and microbial interface biology, bile-acid handling, and gut immune signaling.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Digestion, malabsorption, gut barrier, and microbiome interaction context

research_context: Use this marker as a SI pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.

variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair SI gut-digestion variation model with SI methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
